 {"id":"bd-sfn","title":"E2E: dsr health (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr health (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify host health checks run against real local host data and return schema-valid output.

## Approach
- Use temp hosts.yaml with one local host definition.
- Run `dsr health` and `dsr health check <host>`.

## Logging
- Record hosts config used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and cache files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local host reports healthy (or actionable warnings).
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.
- [ ] Cache behavior does not hide errors.","notes":"E2E test created: scripts/tests/e2e_health.sh - covers all acceptance criteria.  {"id":"bd-v78","title":"Unit tests for toolchain_detect.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for toolchain_detect.sh Module

## Module Overview
toolchain_detect.sh provides safe toolchain detection for installers:
- `toolchain_detect` - Detect specific toolchain (rust/go/bun/node)
- `toolchain_detect_all` - Detect all supported toolchains
- `toolchain_ensure` - Verify toolchain available
- `toolchain_install` - Install if missing (with consent)
- `_tc_version_ge` - Compare semver versions

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Detection Tests
- Detect Rust via `rustc --version`
- Detect Go via `go version`
- Detect Bun via `bun --version`
- Detect Node via `node --version`
- Handle missing toolchain gracefully

### 2. Version Comparison Tests
- 1.70.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.71.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.69.0 >= 1.70.0 → false
- Handle pre-release versions (1.70.0-beta)
- Handle missing version string

### 3. Platform Detection Tests
- Detect linux/amd64 correctly
- Detect darwin/arm64 correctly
- Detect windows/amd64 correctly
- Handle unknown platform

### 4. Installation Tests (with mocks)
- Prompt for consent in interactive mode
- Skip prompt in non-interactive mode
- Never overwrite existing installation
- Log installation commands (dry-run)

### 5. Ensure Tests
- Pass when toolchain present and version OK
- Fail when toolchain missing
- Fail when version too old
- Suggest installation command

## Test Approach
- Mock toolchain commands where needed
- Test version comparison logic directly
- Use NON_INTERACTIVE=true for consent bypass
- Verify no actual installations occur","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.378100130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.377592634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v78","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-z2t","title":"E2E: dsr release (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr release (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate release upload using real gh auth when available; otherwise skip with guidance.

## Approach
- Use a temp artifacts dir with small files and checksums.
- If gh auth present, target a test repo or draft release.
- If gh auth missing, skip with clear steps.

## Logging
- Log repo, tag, artifacts dir.
- On failure, dump gh output and stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Draft release created and assets uploaded when auth present.
- [ ] Dry-run path produces deterministic plan output.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:54.772796976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:34:44.370102301Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:34:44.370085119Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z2t","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:38.810809374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z2t","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:40.415849071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -2746,6 +2746,35 @@ EOF
         return 0
     }
 
+    _release_arch_aliases() {
+        local name="$1"
+        local arch="$2"
+        local -a aliases=()
+
+        case "$arch" in
+            amd64)
+                if [[ "$name" == *"amd64"* ]]; then
+                    local alias
+                    alias="${name//-amd64/-x86_64}"
+                    alias="${alias//_amd64/_x86_64}"
+                    [[ "$alias" != "$name" ]] && aliases+=("$alias")
+                fi
+                ;;
+            arm64)
+                if [[ "$name" == *"arm64"* ]]; then
+                    local alias
+                    alias="${name//-arm64/-aarch64}"
+                    alias="${alias//_arm64/_aarch64}"
+                    [[ "$alias" != "$name" ]] && aliases+=("$alias")
+                fi
+                ;;
+        esac
+
+        if [[ ${#aliases[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "${aliases[@]}"
+        fi
+    }
+
     # Map manifest artifact names to targets and formats (for dual-name uploads)
     local -A manifest_targets=()
     local -A manifest_formats=()
@@ -2843,6 +2872,22 @@ EOF
                     if [[ -n "$compat_name" && "$compat_name" != "$filename" && "$compat_name" != "$versioned_name" ]]; then
                         upload_names+=("$compat_name")
                     fi
+                    if [[ -n "$compat_name" ]]; then
+                        while IFS= read -r alias_name; do
+                            [[ -z "$alias_name" ]] && continue
+                            local seen_alias=false
+                            local existing
+                            for existing in "${upload_names[@]}"; do
+                                if [[ "$existing" == "$alias_name" ]]; then
+                                    seen_alias=true
+                                    break
+                                fi
+                            done
+                            if ! $seen_alias; then
+                                upload_names+=("$alias_name")
+                            fi
+                        done < <(_release_arch_aliases "$compat_name" "$arch")
+                    fi
 
                     log_info "[$idx/$total_files] Uploading asset variants for: $filename"

@@ -146,11 +146,25 @@ _json_result() {
                 --arg path "$path" \
                 '{tool: $tool, status: $status, message: $message, version: $version, path: $path}'
         else
-            # Fallback for systems without jq - escape quotes manually
-            local esc_msg="${message//\"/\\\"}"
-            local esc_path="${path//\"/\\\"}"
+            # Fallback for systems without jq - escape JSON special characters
+            # Order matters: escape backslashes first, then quotes, then control chars
+            _json_escape_str() {
+                local s="$1"
+                s="${s//\\/\\\\}"      # \ -> \\
+                s="${s//\"/\\\"}"      # " -> \"
+                s="${s//$'\n'/\\n}"    # newline -> \n
+                s="${s//$'\t'/\\t}"    # tab -> \t
+                s="${s//$'\r'/\\r}"    # carriage return -> \r
+                printf '%s' "$s"
+            }
+            local esc_tool esc_status esc_msg esc_ver esc_path
+            esc_tool=$(_json_escape_str "$TOOL_NAME")
+            esc_status=$(_json_escape_str "$status")
+            esc_msg=$(_json_escape_str "$message")
+            esc_ver=$(_json_escape_str "$version")
+            esc_path=$(_json_escape_str "$path")
             printf '{"tool":"%s","status":"%s","message":"%s","version":"%s","path":"%s"}\n' \
-                "$TOOL_NAME" "$status" "$esc_msg" "$version" "$esc_path"
+                "$esc_tool" "$esc_status" "$esc_msg" "$esc_ver" "$esc_path"
         fi
     fi
 }
